danger_signals.visual_form: Depleted health bar; character visual damage/wear
danger_signals.meaning: Fighter approaching defeat; desperation moves become available

danger_signals.visual_form: Ki meter drain during special move execution
danger_signals.meaning: Limited special attack capability; vulnerability window

danger_signals.visual_form: Character sent to back of stage via opponent attack
danger_signals.meaning: Loss of position advantage; increased distance to opponent

danger_signals.visual_form: Both fighters strike simultaneously; visual clash effect
danger_signals.meaning: Mutual attack moment; Ki blast counterattack window triggered

safety_signals.meaning: Fighter at peak condition; maximum combat capability

safety_signals.visual_form: Ki meter increasing; character glowing aura effect
safety_signals.meaning: Safe to execute special moves; power building

safety_signals.visual_form: Character positioned at center/forward stage area
safety_signals.meaning: Offensive positioning advantage; closer to opponent

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Goku, Gohan, Gotenks, Piccolo represent protagonist/hero faction
Frieza, Perfect Cell, Majin Buu, Kid Buu, Majin Vegeta represent antagonist faction
Vegeta (multiple forms including Majin variant), Vegito (fusion form) represent ambiguous alignment

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Character roster includes Frieza, establishing early Dragon Ball Z timeline
Roster spans Frieza through Majin Buu saga conclusion; Perfect Cell, Majin Buu, Kid Buu presence
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Progression through major story arcs reflected in available characters

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Character inclusion reflects power hierarchy: Frieza → Cell → Buu forms
Majin Vegeta, Kid Buu, and Vegito represent alternate/evolved forms; visual distinction from base forms
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Different transformation states available as distinct roster entries

environmental_storytelling.example: Ten-character roster spanning Frieza through Majin Buu sagas communicates game's story scope and timeline coverage
Heroes (Goku, Gohan) versus villains (Frieza, Cell, Buu) matchups inherently communicate narrative antagonism without dialog
Majin Vegeta and Majin Buu variants indicate corruption/transformation events; Kid Buu indicates final saga state
Vegito presence signals ultimate fusion technique narrative event; represents protagonist power escalation

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Desperation moves become available; visual strain/damage indicators on sprite
Special move execution possible; character aura intensifies
Ki blast counterattack triggered; mutual damage exchange mechanic activates
Stage distance increases; must close gap to resume close-range offense

notes: Dragon Ball Z: Hyper Dimension is a one-on-one fighting game with minimal narrative communication beyond character roster selection. Danger/safety signals are primarily mechanical (health/Ki bars) rather than environmental. Faction indicators are derived from source material canon (hero vs. villain designation) rather than in-game visual language. The game communicates world-state primarily through character availability and transformation variants, reflecting the chronological scope from Frieza saga through Majin Buu saga conclusion. No explicit political situation communication systems detected in available source material.
